description_long: This is a medium-sized crab with a robust, rounded carapace and a pair of prominent, asymmetrical claws, one of which is significantly larger than the other. The carapace is dark brown to gray, with subtle mottling and a slightly rough texture, while the legs are slender and jointed, tapering to pointed tips. The claws are pale, almost white, with the larger claw displaying a pronounced curve and a broad, flattened surface. The eyes are set on short stalks at the front of the carapace, providing a wide field of vision. The underside and joints of the legs show lighter, yellowish hues, contrasting with the darker dorsal surfaces. This crab's overall appearance suggests it is adapted for both defense and burrowing, with sturdy limbs and a hard exoskeleton.
description: A robust crab with a dark, rounded carapace, prominent asymmetrical claws, and slender, jointed legs. The surface is mottled brown and gray with pale claws and eyes on short stalks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's face, both claws (with the left claw raised and larger), and the eyes on stalks.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the large, curved claw and the slender, jointed legs extending backward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded carapace, symmetrical arrangement of legs, and both claws visible from behind.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the smaller claw and the crab's body profile, with legs splayed for support.
